chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Crl. Appeal No.S-69 of 2022 Crl. Appeal No.S-79 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. 1.For orders on MA 4347/2022 2.For hearing of main case 11-12-2023 M/s Syed Zafar Ali Shah and Athar Hussain Abro, Advocates for appellants in Crl. Appeal No.S-69 of 2022. Mr. Ubedullah Ghoto, Advocate for appellants in Crl. Appeal No.S-79 of 2022 and for complainant in Crl. Appeal No.S-69 of 2022. Mr. Aftab Ahmed Shar, Additional P.G for the State. ***** Both these appeals are partly heard. But the parties have filed relevant applications under sections 345 and 345 CrPC seeking compromise in the case, and so also appointment of Walis on behalf of minors of deceased in both the matters and have made a request that these applications may be transmitted to the trial Court for verification and ascertaining the genuineness of the compromise between the parties and recording of their statements in this case after finding out all legal heirs left by the deceased. Learned counsel further submit that ladies, who are also legal heirs of both the families are veil observing (pardanasheen ladies) and they do not come out of their Havelies, hence trial Court may be directed to depute concerned Judicial Magistrate for recording statements of ladies of both the families in their houses after due verification. The trial Court may also be directed to call the accused from jail for the purpose of obtaining their signatures/thumb impressions and confirming the factum of compromise between the parties. Learned Additional P.G has recorded no objection. Accordingly, office is directed to transmit these applications after allotting numbers to the trial Court for the purpose, as detailed above, in addition to designating some Judicial Magistrate for recording statements of ladies

Learned Additional P.G has recorded no objection. Accordingly, office is directed to transmit these applications after allotting numbers to the trial Court for the purpose, as detailed above, in addition to designating some Judicial Magistrate for recording statements of ladies 2 who are found legal heirs of deceased in their houses after confirming their identity. Let this exercise be completed as soon as possible, preferably within a period of one month and report be submitted to this Court. Adjourned to a date in office. Office to place a signed copy of this order in captioned connected matter. JUDGE Ahmad
